channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Congress unveils funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders have unveiled a stopgap spending bill that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: After investigating Jan. 6, House GOP sides with Trump and goes after Liz Cheney
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Wrapping up their own investigation on the Jan. 6 2021 Capitol attack, House Republicans have concluded it's former GOP Rep.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats pick Gerry Connolly for Oversight post, rejecting Ocasio-Cortez bid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Democrats on Tuesday picked 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly to lead the party next year atop an influential congressional committee, pushing aside growing calls for generational change in leadership ahead of a second term for Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Horsford vows to defend Nevada families as priority for government cuts grows
item.description: Nevada Democratic Rep. Steven Horsford is doing more than just serving as a watchdog on Republican initiatives to cut government bureaucracy -- he's participating in the process.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Stadium site provision included in federal spending bill, a win for the Commanders and NFL
item.description: The Washington Commanders are another step closer to the possibility of returning to the franchise’s old home in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawmakers eye 'low hanging fruit' for government efficiency after first DOGE Caucus meeting
item.description: The Congressional DOGE Caucus had its first meeting on Tuesday, and lawmakers left hopeful about what is to come.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics: Cheney Accused of 'Potential' Witness Tampering
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, featuring the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s lawyers allege juror misconduct in latest bid to get his hush money conviction dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers are raising a new claim in their fight to overturn his hush money conviction, alleging that the historic verdict was tainted by juror misconduct.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas school districts asked to return $16 million in federal funding for special education services
item.description: The request comes after an audit found Texas improperly billed the federal government for reimbursements related to Medicaid-eligible students.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: Jill Biden says she has closed the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden has closed the book on her teaching career at a Virginia community college. The first lady, who has spent the past 40 years teaching in classrooms, announced Monday that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College last week.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump seeks to smooth over concerns over RFK Jr
item.description: “I think he’s going to be much less radical than you would think,” Trump said during a wide-ranging news conference at Mar-a-Lago.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Angie Craig chosen as top Democrat on U.S House Agriculture Committee
item.description: Minnesota 2nd District Rep. Angie Craig was elected Tuesday to serve as the top Democrat on the U.S. House Agriculture Committee.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Federal agents, hazmat sent to home of Ohio Secretary of State
item.description: Multiple agencies were sent to Ohio Secretary of State Frank Larose's residence for a reported "security incident.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, NewsNation

item.title: Government funding bill will run through March, include $100B in disaster aid
item.description: Pieces of a bipartisan effort to prevent a government shutdown during the holiday season are beginning to come into focus, including a March end date and $100 billion in disaster aid. GOP leadership briefed Republicans on Tuesday morning on some of the details of the forthcoming legislation.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Freedom Caucus reelects Andy Harris as chair
item.description: Rep. Andy Harris (R-Md.) will chair the House Freedom Caucus through the end of 2025, members of the group decided Monday night, cementing his leadership of the hard-line conservative caucus.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two charged in connection with Iran-backed drone strike that killed 3 US troops in the Middle East
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Two men, including a dual Iranian American citizen, have been charged with conspiring to export sensitive technology to Iran that was used in a drone attack in Jordan that killed three American troops early this year and injured dozens of other service members, the Justice Department s
item.dc:creator: STEVE LEBLANC, ERIC TUCKER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Senator Schumer urges use of ROBIN to track mystery drones
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sen. Chuck Schumer is making a plea for federal officials to launch a declassified special drone detection system called the ROBIN – a 360-degree technology across New York, New Jersey and the metro area to track any unidentified object in the sky.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams' Chief Advisor Ingrid Lewis-Martin steps down
item.description: Editor's Note: The video above originally aired on Sept. 25, 2024. NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ Chief Advisor Ingrid Lewis-Martin has stepped down, sources told PIX11 News. Lewis-Martin on Sunday announced she's leaving her post.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: "Speaker Pelosi is enjoying the overwhelming outpouring of prayers and well wishes," Ian Krager said.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Trump's presidential library to settle lawsuit
item.description: ABC News agreed to pay $15 million to Trump's presidential library to settle a lawsuit over George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that Trump was found liable for raping E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz reflects on Democrats’ shortcomings, return to Minnesota as political ground shifts
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is back at the Capitol and reacclimating to life after his vice presidential bid. Politics Friday host Brian Bakst and senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson unpack an interview with Walz about three months on the national stage and what awaits next year.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Dana Ferguson, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to next month's inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is breaking with precedent in inviting a foreign head of state to his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis City Council overrides mayor’s veto of 2025 budget
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council enacted its 2025 budget on Thursday, after overturning a veto by Mayor Jacob Frey.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences for 1,500 people, the largest act of clemency in a day
item.description: The 1,500 people had been serving long prison sentences that would have been shorter under today's laws and practices. Three Minnesota women are among those to get their federal convictions pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith and Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Lombardo joins GOP governors 'fully committed' to Trump deportation threats
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo and 25 other Republican governors lined up in support of President-elect Donald Trump's immigration policies on Wednesday, calling for the deportation of "individuals who pose a threat.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
